cityDNA.coreFantasy: A Indian / South Asian city where Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Records / Archives, Medical / Healing, and Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter turn Riverside, Floodplain, and Canal Basin, River, and Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, and River Blessing into civic order.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Suryavaad should feel like a fully usable civilization: its landmarks are not decoration, but the places where movement, memory, authority, labor, and public identity become visible.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Suryavaad organizes itself through Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Records / Archives, Medical / Healing, and Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter. The city’s visible grammar should always connect landscape, materials, daily work, and civic magic rather than letting any one element become generic fantasy dressing.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Teak, Red Brick, Limestone Blocks, Silk, and Arcane Glow; Riverside, Floodplain, and Canal Basin; River; section landmarks such as Lock House Beacon, Grain Hall, Archive Tower, Healing Hall, and Ancestor Vault; and the city-defining trio of Indian Lotus Stepwell of the Seven Lamps, Lock House Beacon, and Lotus Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Suryavaad belongs to a Indian / South Asian population whose public life should be expressed through architecture, movement, civic ritual, tools, housing, food, and body language rather than only costume or ornament.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Indian Lotus Stepwell of the Seven Lamps expresses the citywide mythic identity; Lock House Beacon expresses public warning and convergence; Lotus Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court expresses the civic legitimacy of District 13.

canonLocks: Suryavaad is a Indian / South Asian city; its identity must be visible through built form, street life, and civic use.
The Wonder is Indian Lotus Stepwell of the Seven Lamps.
The Beacon is Lock House Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Lotus Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court and belongs to central District 13.
The five section identities are: S1 Canal / Riverworks, S2 Granary / Food Market, S3 Records / Archives, S4 Medical / Healing, S5 Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter.
S1 preserves the source-backed anchor Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall (CIVIC_CENTER).
S1 preserves the source-backed anchor Lock House Beacon (MAJOR_LANDMARK).
S1 preserves the source-backed anchor Canal Reflections Monument (MONUMENT).
S1 preserves the source-backed anchor Waterwheel Court Nook (MINOR_LANDMARK).
S2 preserves the source-backed anchor Granary / Food Market Civic Hall (CIVIC_CENTER).
S2 preserves the source-backed anchor Grain Hall (MAJOR_LANDMARK).
S2 preserves the source-backed anchor Awning Monument (MONUMENT).
S2 preserves the source-backed anchor Mill Court Nook (MINOR_LANDMARK).
S3 preserves the source-backed anchor Records / Archives Hall (CIVIC_CENTER).
S3 preserves the source-backed anchor Archive Tower (MAJOR_LANDMARK).
S3 preserves the source-backed anchor Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument (MONUMENT).
S3 preserves the source-backed anchor Copyist Hall Nook (MINOR_LANDMARK).
S4 preserves the source-backed anchor Medical / Healing Civic Hall (CIVIC_CENTER).
S4 preserves the source-backed anchor Healing Hall (MAJOR_LANDMARK).
S4 preserves the source-backed anchor Medical Healing Lantern Monument (MONUMENT).
S4 preserves the source-backed anchor Apothecary Court Nook (MINOR_LANDMARK).
S5 preserves the source-backed anchor Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall (CIVIC_CENTER).
S5 preserves the source-backed anchor Ancestor Vault (MAJOR_LANDMARK).
S5 preserves the source-backed anchor Ancestor Masks Monument (MONUMENT).
S5 preserves the source-backed anchor Bone Lantern Path Nook (MINOR_LANDMARK).
provisionalCanon: Suryavaad's civic customs should be inferred from source-backed section functions: Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Records / Archives, Medical / Healing, and Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter.
Local politics likely emerge from the tension between public service, landmark access, and the authority of D13, but specific factions should not be invented here.
rejectedReads: Suryavaad as a generic fantasy city with interchangeable districts.
Landmarks as empty decoration rather than civic tools.
Race or culture identity expressed only through clothing while architecture and institutions remain generic.
Invented street anchors for any missing street section.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact names for Wonder, Beacon, Seat of Power, section civic halls, section landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
Do not add character-specific observations to the city analysis.
Keep every district usable for prose, diorama generation, and map background generation.
Use uncertainty only in metadata or provisional canon; do not pad missing evidence with speculation.

water: water infrastructure visible through Canal / Riverworks
climateLighting: daylight that makes civic silhouettes board-readable
civic blue illumination at signal or authority points
localized lighting shaped by Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, and River Blessing
street shadow under arcades, halls, platforms, or canopy forms
environment.environmentalLogic: Suryavaad's environment should be written as infrastructure. Riverside, Floodplain, and Canal Basin and River determine how citizens move, where crowds gather, where authority can be seen, and how each section feels under pressure.

skyline: Indian Lotus Stepwell of the Seven Lamps as the far citywide wonder silhouette.
Lotus Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court as the central D13 authority mass.
Lock House Beacon as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks remain subordinate to the Wonder and Seat but must be readable: Lock House Beacon, Grain Hall, Archive Tower, Healing Hall, and Ancestor Vault.
lighting: civic blue illumination at beacon and authority points
warm practical light from shops and homes
Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, and River Blessing expressed as restrained civic effect rather than spectacle alone
colorLanguage: colors derived from Teak, Red Brick, Limestone Blocks, Silk, and Arcane Glow
civic blue for signal, oath, or authority moments
warm street colors for shops, homes, and food stalls
section-specific accent colors tied to landmark function
textureLanguage: worked textures of Teak, Red Brick, Limestone Blocks, Silk, and Arcane Glow
worn public paving at district approaches
handled wood, cloth, reed, stone, or metal at street scale
official surfaces at civic halls, D13 courts, and named landmarks

civilization.people: Suryavaad's people are Indian / South Asian. They should move, gesture, dress, work, and gather according to the city’s section functions rather than appearing as generic crowds.
socialClasses: D13 officials, registry staff, judges, or civic command families
section civic-hall administrators and landmark attendants
craft, trade, food, route, shrine, archive, or service guild families
street vendors, apprentices, runners, porters, and maintenance workers
households whose status is legible through proximity to named landmarks and public approaches
civilization.governance: Governance is centered on Lotus Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, but citizens experience authority through section halls, permit counters, procession routes, market rules, warning signals, and the public visibility of the Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religion and ritual should emerge through section evidence: sacred groves, shrines, temples, ancestor quarters, vows, processions, lamps, memorials, or civic magic where source-backed.
customs: citizens orient by named landmarks rather than abstract street addresses
public notices and civic signals are treated as social facts
section identity shapes work rhythms, festival routes, food habits, and greetings
households preserve small visual signs of their relation to nearby halls, shrines, markets, or work yards
crowds read beacon changes, D13 messengers, and landmark access as political weather
bodyLanguage: habitual glances toward the Beacon or D13 when public pressure rises
rank-conscious spacing near halls, courts, gates, and counters
work gestures shaped by tools, ledgers, baskets, lamps, platforms, or ritual objects
section-specific ways of queuing, bargaining, processing, mourning, or carrying goods
practical familiarity with terraces, arcades, courtyards, and side lanes
speechPatterns: citizens reference exact landmark names in directions and gossip
official speech uses seals, sections, district numbers, and public obligations
market and work speech remains compressed, local, and landmark-aware
ceremonial speech becomes formal near Wonder, Beacon, Seat, temple, shrine, or ancestor anchors
outsiders are corrected when they genericize a named civic anchor

streets.appearance: Streets should foreground Teak, Red Brick, Limestone Blocks, Silk, and Arcane Glow, section-specific props, named local landmarks, D13 authority sightlines, and the daily relationship between Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Records / Archives, Medical / Healing, and Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter.
streets.movement: Movement follows district adjacency and section identity: S1 through S5 each have five district slots, while D13 remains the central seat-of-power node around Lotus Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
sounds: foot traffic shifting around landmark approaches
vendors, clerks, attendants, or guards calling section-specific instructions
tools, carts, bells, lamps, water, banners, or crowds depending on section function
distant beacon reaction when civic signals change
private household sounds from side lanes behind public halls
smells: worked city materials under sun or lantern heat
lamp oil, damp stone, dust, reed, wood, incense, metal, or archive paper according to section
vegetation or water smells where source-backed
streets.lighting: Use practical street light first: daylight, shade, lanterns, and civic blue accents. Magic should reinforce civic identity rather than overtake every street.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should change how crowds read landmarks: glare, rain, mist, dust, cold, humidity, wind, or wet paving can hide, reveal, or delay public action.
streets.dayCycle: Morning emphasizes food, work, maintenance, permission-seeking, trade preparation, ritual cleaning, and civic notices.
streets.nightCycle: Night shifts to lamps, quieter side courts, guarded landmarks, late markets, household thresholds, watch routines, and beacon visibility.

common: food stalls shaped by local staples
basket, crate, reed, stone, timber, or metal workers
uncommon: specialist map, ritual, archive, or route counters
prestigious: high-status court, archive, shrine, or civic contractors
exports: goods using Teak, Red Brick, Limestone Blocks, Silk, and Arcane Glow
craft traditions tied to Indian / South Asian civic identity
section-specific tools, records, ritual goods, food goods, or transport services
recognizable landmark miniatures, seals, lamps, textiles, or carved emblems
imports: rare pigments, spices, glass, metal fittings, fine timber, ceremonial cloth, and replacement tools
raw materials not available in local terrain
foods and ritual supplies needed for section festivals
streetFood: tea, fermented drinks, or cooling drinks
section-specific food sold near civic halls

housing.wealthy: Large homes cluster near central authority, prestigious landmark approaches, garden courts, or high terraces, with ornament that repeats city motifs without overwhelming civic structures.
housing.middle: Shop-homes, courtyard houses, upper rooms, workshop apartments, and shared terraces connect directly to section work rhythms.
housing.poor: Small rooms, backstreet courts, temporary structures, and service-side housing remain visually tied to the city through lamps, fabrics, recycled materials, and landmark-facing orientation.

districts.name: Canal / Riverworks — Civic Threshold
districts.civicPurpose: This district handles public approach, first contact, notices, and movement control for Canal / Riverworks. In scenes, it should show how water access, movement control, maintenance, arrival rituals, and civic dependence on managed flows becomes a street-level system.
districts.visualIdentity: Canal / Riverworks identity rendered through sluice wheels, water gauges, wet stone, reed edges, Teak, Red Brick, Limestone Blocks, Silk, and Arcane Glow, local crowds, and the visible approach to Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall.
gameplayAssetNeeds: Indian / South Asian civilians using the district
clear board-readable pathing and gathering space
narrativeUse: stage canal / riverworks scenes without needing to reopen concept art
show how ordinary citizens react to public pressure
give characters a concrete approach, obstacle, or meeting point
translate citywide crisis into district-scale behavior
support diorama generation with landmark-specific props

districts.visualIdentity: Medical / Healing identity rendered through civic signs, public lamps, side courts, local kiosks, Teak, Red Brick, Limestone Blocks, Silk, and Arcane Glow, local crowds, and the visible approach to Healing Hall.
gameplayAssetNeeds: Indian / South Asian civilians using the district
clear board-readable pathing and gathering space
narrativeUse: stage medical / healing scenes without needing to reopen concept art
show how ordinary citizens react to public pressure
give characters a concrete approach, obstacle, or meeting point
translate citywide crisis into district-scale behavior
support diorama generation with landmark-specific props

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 dioramas must read as Canal / Riverworks and include source-backed anchors: Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House Beacon, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook.
S2 dioramas must read as Granary / Food Market and include source-backed anchors: Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, Mill Court Nook.
S3 dioramas must read as Records / Archives and include source-backed anchors: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook.
S4 dioramas must read as Medical / Healing and include source-backed anchors: Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook.
S5 dioramas must read as Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter and include source-backed anchors: Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, Ancestor Vault, Ancestor Masks Monument, Bone Lantern Path Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show Suryavaad as a Indian / South Asian city shaped by Riverside, Floodplain, and Canal Basin and River.
Keep Indian Lotus Stepwell of the Seven Lamps distinct as the skyline wonder, Lotus Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court central at D13, and Lock House Beacon legible as the public signal.
Arrange the map so the five section identities remain readable: Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Records / Archives, Medical / Healing, and Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter.
Do not let generic fantasy rooftops replace the named civic anchors.
Use paths, plazas, courts, water edges, walls, terraces, or arcades to show how citizens move between sections.
requiredVisualElements: Indian Lotus Stepwell of the Seven Lamps
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or production notes in the JSON
generic city views that ignore section identity
invented landmarks not present in sidecars or established canon
making the Wonder, Beacon, and Seat interchangeable
overusing magic effects until practical civic infrastructure disappears
